fields.name: Minimal deployment time

fields.description: The system shall have impact sensors in the front side and back of the car.
fields.name: Impact sensors

fields.description: The system shall have an airbag control unit whichmonitors the readiness of the entire airbag system. It poles the system at each startup and if a fault is found will illuminate the Air Bag light and set a code in the computer's memory. This module will also provide backup power in case the battery is disconnected. Always refer to the manufacturer's documentation before to repair any part of an airbag system. Air bag systems must be disconnected for a certain period of time before any work can be .　変更
fields.name: ACU

fields.description: The system shall respond to impacts at the front, side and back of the vehicle
fields.name: Impact response

fields.description: The system shall not deploy when there are less than 10 lbs in the passenger seat.
fields.name: Passenger weight detection
Seat sensor failure requirements

fields.description: The item shall be developed with two seat sensors located under the front driver and passenger seats
fields.name: Seat Sensor Location

fields.description: The output of the two seat sensors shall be compared so that if a difference of 5lb between the sensors when the vehicle is empty, a signal shall be sent to the ACU module
fields.name: Compare Seat Sensor Output

fields.description: The result of the signal from the arbitration unit shall be received by the ACU module and it shall force the item into a safe state and warn the driver of the failure
fields.name: Seat Sensor Failure Action

fields.description: A watchdog running at 2 Khz shall be used by the ACU module to determine if the seat sensor subsystem fails due to loss of power
fields.name: Seat Sensor Watchdog
Speed sensor failure requirements

fields.description: Two independent means of sensing vehicle speed information shall be developed
fields.name: Independent Speed Sensing

fields.description: The two speed signals shall be compared and if they differ greater than +/-5% over a period of 5 samples (running at 5 Hz) then a signal indicating a speed sensor failure shall be sent to the ACU module
fields.name: Speed Sensor Failure Detection

fields.description: When the ACU module recieves the speed sensor failure signal it shall put itself into a safe state and warn the driver of the sensor failure
fields.name: Speed Sensor Failure Action

fields.description: A watchdog running at 2 Khz shall be used by the ACU module to determine if the Speed sensor subsystem fails due to loss of power
fields.name: Speed Sensor Watchdog
Communications failures

fields.description: An independently powered, dual channel bus system shall be used to ensure a redundancy of information being passed between all the susbsystems
fields.name: Redundant Bus System

fields.description: The information from both independent bus systems shall be compared to determine that the signals are not different to within a range of +/-3%.
fields.name: Redundant Signal Comparison

fields.description: If a difference between signals on the communication channels is detected then a warning signal shall be indicated to the driver and an error code captured
fields.name: Communications Failure Action

fields.description: The switch to initiate the ACU shall be passed over both bus systems to ensure that if the signal fails then it is captured by the bus system signal comparator
fields.name: ACU Enable Routing
Impact sensor failure requirements

fields.description: A watchdog running at 2 Khz shall be used by the ACU module to determine if the impact sensor subsystem fails due to loss of power
fields.name: Impact Sensor Watchdog

fields.description: Two independent means of sensing vehicle impact information shall be developed
fields.name: Independent Impact Sensing

fields.description: The two impact signals shall be compared and if they differ greater than +/-5% over a period of 5 samples (running at 5 Hz) then a signal indicating an impact sensor failure shall be sent to the ACU module
fields.name: Impact Sensor Failure Detection

fields.description: When the ACU module recieves the impact sensor failure signal it shall put itself into a safe state and warn the driver of the sensor failure
fields.name: Impact Sensor Failure Action
Timing constraints

fields.description: When a signal corruption is detected by the ACU, the Airbag shall be switched off within 100 ms and the driver shall be appropriately notified
fields.name: Signal Corruption Detect Time

fields.description: When airbag signal corruption is detected by the ACU, the system shall be switched off within 50 ms and the driver shall be appropriately notified
fields.name: Airbag Signal Corruption Detect Time

fields.description: When vehicle speed corruption is detected by the ACU, the cruise control shall be switched off within 50 ms.
fields.name: Vehicle Speed Corruption Detect Time
Roll rate sensor failure requirements

fields.description: Two independent means of sensing vehicle roll reate information shall be developed
fields.name: Independent roll sensing

fields.description: The two roll rate signals shall be compared and if they differ greater than +/-5% over a period of 5 samples (running at 5 Hz) then a signal indicating a roll rate sensor failure shall be sent to the ACU module
fields.name: Roll sensor failure detection

fields.description: When the ACU module receives the roll rate sensor failure signal it shall put itself into a safe state and warn the driver of the sensor failure
fields.name: Roll sensor failure action

fields.description: A watchdog running at 2 Khz shall be used by the ACU module to determine if the roll rate sensor subsystem fails due to loss of power
fields.name: Roll sensor watchdog

fields.description: The passenger seat shall be able to detect weight at multiple points using redundant weight sensors to handle unusual displacement of passenger weight. The goal of this requirement is to mitigate the potential risk of the passenger airbag turning off when there is unusual weight displacement.
fields.name: Ability to detect weight under unusual weight displacement on seat
Architecture Diagram (Image)

fields.description: The sensor shall use a 2 wire current interface to communicate with the ECU
fields.name: 2 wire current interface

fields.description: The sensor shall have an impact measurement range of 220g
fields.name: Measurement Range

fields.description: Validate the the airbag deploys in under 30 milliseconds
fields.name: Deploys in under 30 milliseconds

testCaseSteps.action: the bumper touches the crash wall at the time t=0
after approximately 25 ms the electronic sensor activates the squib for the driver module
after 30 ms the cover has already been torn off the driver module to allow the airbag to inflate
the driver airbag is fully inflated after around 55 ms and the driver dives in
after around 65 ms the passenger-side airbag is also fully inflated and the front-seat passenger also falls into the airbag
after around 85 ms the driver has reached maximum immersion in the bag and moves away from the steering wheel again
the front-seat passenger reaches the foremost position after 100 ms and then starts to move back
after 150 ms the accident is over, the passengers are back in their starting positions and both airbags are empty to a large extent.

fields.description:
ASIL - An Automotive Safety Integrity Level (ASIL) represents an automotive-specific risk-based classification of a safety goal as well as the validation and confirmation measures required by the standard to ensure accomplishment of that goal.

 

Hazards - Potential source of harm caused by malfunctioning behavior of the item definition. ASIL is defined at the hazard level and all items decomposed from that hazards shall inherit the ASIL.

 

Item Descriptions - Item Definition is used to refer to a specific system or array of systems that implements a function at the vehicle level to which the ISO 26262 Safety Life Cycle is applied. That is, the item definition is the highest identified object in the process and is thereby the starting point for product-specific safety development under this standard.

 

Requirement - A property or capability that must be achieved or possessed by a system, system item, product, or service to satisfy a contract, standard, specification or other formally imposed documents. If a requirement has an ASIL other than QM, it shall be treated as a Safety Requirement.

 

Safety Goal - A safety goal is a top-level safety requirement that is assigned to a system, with the purpose of reducing the risk of one or more hazardous events to a tolerable level.

 

Safety Requirement - Safety requirements include all safety goals and all levels of requirements decomposed from the safety goals down to and including the lowest level of functional and technical safety requirements allocated to hardware and software components.

 

Stakeholder Requirements - requirements and requests through direct solicitation of customer input and through review of customer business proposals (where relevant), target operating and hardware environment, and other documents bearing on customer requirements.

 

System - A collection of interacting items organized to accomplish a specific function or set of functions within a specific environment.

 

Use Case - A use case is a list of actions or event steps, typically defining the interactions between a role or actor and a system, to achieve a goal. The actor can be a human, an external system, or time. In systems engineering, use cases are used at a higher level than within software engineering, often representing missions or stakeholder goals.

 

 

User Story - A user story is a description consisting of one or more sentences in the everyday or business language of the end user or user of a system that captures what a user does or needs to do as part of his or her job function. It captures the "who", "what" and "why" of a requirement in a simple, concise way, often limited in detail by what can be hand-written on a small paper notecard.

 

Validation - Validation demonstrates that the work item can be used by the users for their specific tasks.

 

Verification - Verification is confirmation, through the provision of objective evidence, that specified requirements have been fulfilled in a given work item.

fields.description: Validate that the airbag shuts off at speeds < 10 mph
fields.name: shuts off at speeds < 10 mph

testCaseSteps.action: accelerate to 9 mph
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag is in the off state

testCaseSteps.action: accelerate to 10 mph
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag is in the on state

testCaseSteps.action: decelerate to 9 mph
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag is in the off state

fields.description: To simulate dynamic acceleration during driving on a curve, the sensor is checked by gravitational acceleration under static conditions. The method involves positioning the vehicle on a transverse ramp with known angle of inclination and comparing the acceleration force measured by the lateral-acceleration sensor with a preset sensor value determined on the basis of the angle of inclination of the ramp.
fields.name: Detect accurate lateral acceleration

testCaseSteps.action: positioning the vehicle on a transverse ramp with known angle of inclination
compare acceleration force measured by the lateral-acceleration sensor with the preset sensor value

fields.description:
The formula for calculating breaking speed is derived from the motion equation:

 

v is the final speed of the vehicle which will be zero, vo will be the initial speed of the vehicle, a is the acceleration (or deceleration) of the vehicle and d is the breaking distance.
fields.name: Detect accurate acceleration

testCaseSteps.action: accelerate to 10 mph
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: sensor reads 10 mph

testCaseSteps.action: fully break to 0 mph
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: sensor reads 0 pmh

fields.description: To simulate dynamic angular velocity of a rolling automobile, the sensor is checked by the acceleration vector under static conditions. The acceleration vector of a fixed reference point on the automobile, moving within an inertial frame, consists of the acceleration of the origin of the body-fixed reference frame and the tangential and centripetal accelerations at the reference point arising from rotation of the the automobile
fields.name: Detect accurate angular velocity

testCaseSteps.action: positioning the vehicle on a flat surface
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: check against known static conditions are unchanged

testCaseSteps.action: rotate the vehicle by 10 degrees
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: compare acceleration force measured by the angular velocity sensor with the preset sensor value. A change should be noted

fields.description:
Each sensor is part of a two sensor system. If their is a failure in a sensor due to power outage or other anomalies, the other sensor will continue to work. When both sensors are functional, each sensor checks itself against the other with a variance of 1ms.

 

If a sensor has a failure, a dashboard warning should be set notifying the driver to have the vehicle checked.
fields.name: Detect accurate response from impact sensors

testCaseSteps.action: turn of sensor 1
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: dashboard warning should be visible

testCaseSteps.action: turn off both sensors
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: dashboard warning should be visible

testCaseSteps.action: turn on both sensors
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: no dashboard warning visible

testCaseSteps.action: conduct front bumper impact with both sensors active
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: response from sensors should have a variance of 1ms or less

testCaseSteps.action: conduct rear bumper impact with both sensors active
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: response from sensors should have a variance of 1ms or less

testCaseSteps.action: conduct driver side impact with both sensors active
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: response from sensors should have a variance of 1ms or less

testCaseSteps.action: conduct passenger side impact with both sensors active
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: response from sensors should have a variance of 1ms or less

fields.description:
Each sensor is part of a two sensor system. If their is a failure in a sensor due to power outage or other anomalies, the other sensor will continue to work. When both sensors are functional, each sensor checks itself against the other with a variance of 1ms.

 

If a sensor has a failure, a dashboard warning should be set notifying the driver to have the vehicle checked.
fields.name: Detect failure in impact sensors

testCaseSteps.action: cut electrical to one front impact sensor
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: dashboard warning should be visible. secondary sensor should still give impact reading

testCaseSteps.action: cut electrical to both front impact sensors
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: dashboard warning should be visible.

testCaseSteps.action: remove one front impact sensor

testCaseSteps.action: remove both front impact sensors

testCaseSteps.expectedResult: dashboard warning should be visible. secondary sensor should still give impact reading

testCaseSteps.action: remove both rear impact sensors
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: dashboard warning should be visible.

fields.description:
The rear safety child seat sensor should accurately detect a child sitting in a child seat in the back passenger seats.

 

This measure should be based on a weight of more than 10 lbs and less than 50 lbs.

 

The sensor should automatically disable the rear airbag and enable dashboard warning of child in.
fields.name: Detect child in safety seat rear passenger side seat

testCaseSteps.action: put 5 lb safety certified child seat in rear passenger side seat
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag remains on. dashboard warning remains off

testCaseSteps.action: put 10 lb sand bag in safety seat
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag turns off. warning light turns on.

testCaseSteps.action: put 20 lb sand bag in safety seat
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag turns off. warning light turns on.

testCaseSteps.action: put 50 lbs sand bag in safety seat
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag stays on. No warning light on dashboard

testCaseSteps.action: put 4 lbs sand bag in safety seat
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: airbag stays off. warning light does not appear.

fields.description: The system shall have an airbag control unit whichmonitors the readiness of the entire airbag system. It poles the system at each startup and if a fault is found will illuminate the Air Bag light and set a code in the computer's memory. This module will also provide backup power in case the battery is disconnected. Always refer to the manufacturer's documentation before attempting to repair any part of an airbag system. Air bag systems must be disconnected for a certain period of time before any work can be performed.
fields.name: ACU

fields.description: The system shall have an inflator assembly incorporated into a module compact enough to fit into a steering wheel, passengers dash or the side of the front seat. The inflator assembly incorporates an electric match that ignites a solid propellant that turns to nitrogen gas, inflating the air bag in milliseconds, faster than the blink of an eye. The powder that comes from the air bag istalcum powder.
fields.name: Inflator

fields.description:
The system shall have impact sensors in the front side and back of the car.

The system shall have lateral acceleration sensors

The system shall have angular velocity sensors

The system shall have seat weight sensors in all driver and passenger seats

The system shall have optical and magnetic speeds sensors on all wheels.
fields.name: Sensors

fields.description: The system shall have an inflatable air bag made of woven nylon.
fields.name: Air Bag

fields.description: The system shall have the ability to turn it on and off manually
fields.name: Manual on/off

fields.description: The system shall have apower supply unit consisting oftwo lines: a 5V-line connected to the microcontroller and the sensors and a 24V-line to the FET- and FASIC-units.
fields.name: Power Supply

fields.description: The system shall have passenger and driver seat belts and seat belt pre-tensioners.
fields.name: Seat Belt

fields.name: Hazard ASIL Reference

fields.description: The airbag is deployed unintentionally, which means that it is ignited even though no crash at all or only a non-critical crash has occurred
fields.name: The airbag is deployed unintentionally

fields.description:
The airbag is not ignited, although a critical crash actually occurred.
This hazard can be formalized as follows:
fields.name: The airbag is not ignited

fields.description: The airbag takes longer that 45 ms to ignite
fields.name: The airbag takes too long to ignite

fields.description: Around 2.5% of all drivers in crashes are likely to suffer serious injury as a result of airbag deployment
fields.name: Broken Bones from rapid inflation

fields.description: Sodium hydroxide is often found in the gas fumes that escape from the airbag as it deflates. Although in only small amounts, the NHTSA has found that sodium hydroxide is causing coughing fits and asthma attacks in the drivers and passengers. Rescue workers are advised to wear gloves and safety glasses and to completely ventilate the car for several minutes before entering. The excessive coughing caused by the air bag powder can also make a chest injury worse.
fields.name: Increased chest injury do to excessive coughing

fields.description: Even though both sensors measure the same signal, the amplitude of this signal at both sensors is different.
fields.name: Differing amplitude of sensors

fields.description:
Both sensor amplitudes have the same value which means that the sensor signals are corrupted, and
  the firing lines of the microcontroller can be set needlessly to high.
fields.name: 5V-line voltage is above the threashold

fields.description: If the voltage is below 4V, then the airbag system will be set to the inactive mode
fields.name: 5V-line is below the threashold

fields.description: A fire command is needlessly sent to the FET and FASIC, thus causing an unintended deployment of the airbag.
fields.name: A fire command is needlessly sent to the FET and FASIC

fields.description: A fire command in case of a critical crash is suppressed, thus preventing the airbag from being ignited.
fields.name: A fire command in case of a critical crash is suppressed

fields.description: Airbag igniting when infant is sitting in front seat
fields.name: Airbag igniting when infant is sitting in front seat

fields.description: The ACU overheats and becomes unresponsive
fields.name: ACU overheats

fields.description: If an error in signalling (within a certain tolerance) are detected the airbag system shall appropriately warn the driver.
fields.name: Signalling Errors

fields.description: The ACU should not cause too much heat due to lack of cooling. Temperatures above  70 C are not allowed.
fields.name: The ACU must not overheat

fields.description: For speeds >10 mph the airbag should be in the activated state
fields.name: airbag activated at speeds over 10 mph

fields.description: The voltage in the ACU shall not be increased above 5 V. A high voltage can damage the ACU and power electronics
fields.name: The voltage in the ACU shall not be increased above 5V

fields.description: The voltage in the ACU shall not fall below 5V. Lower than 5V can cause the ACU to be unresponsive.
fields.name: The voltage in the ACU shall not fall below 5V

fields.description: The system shall detect when the weight of an infant is in the front seat and automatically turn off the airbag
fields.name: Detecting infant in front seat and disabling airbag

fields.description: The airbag shall not deploy when unintended
fields.name: No unintented airbag deployment

fields.description: The airbag propellant shall ignite whenever the airbag inflation is required.
fields.name: Propellant must ignite when airbag ignition is required

fields.description: Inflator medium shall not escape an inflated airbag upon impact.
fields.name: Inflator medium must not escape an inflated airbag on impact

fields.description: The system shall inflate fully and then deflate for both the driver and the passenger in line with standard expectations
fields.name: Safe inflation for driver and passenger

fields.description: Use industry standard inflation recommendations to avoid inflating the airbag for too long or too much in turn potentially causing damage to the passenger
fields.name: Use industry standard Inflation recommendations
